query_normalized: evaluating the use of graph neural networks for anomaly detection in network traffic computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) as it investigates the same algorithmic mechanism (Graph Neural Networks for Anomaly Detection) as the user's question, even though it evaluates on a different graph population (citation networks like Cora/Pubmed) rather than network traffic. It provides necessary methodological context for the GNN-based anomaly detection component of the user's research.
